channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump says Ukraine-Russia talks 'coming to a head' and 'no one is playing' him in push to end war
item.description: PARIS (AP) — President Donald Trump on Friday said negotiations between Ukraine and Russia are “coming to a head” and insisted that neither side is “playing” him in his push to end the grinding war. Trump spoke shortly after Secretary of State Marco Rubio warned that the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ANGELA CHARLTON and HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump’s first quarter approval rating sits at 45 percent: Poll
item.description: Less than half of U.S. adults say they have confidence in the president to recommend or do the right thing for the economy, according to the Gallup poll.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US senator returns from El Salvador trip, says Abrego Garcia case is about far more than one man
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The dispute over the wrongful deportation and imprisonment of Kilmar Abrego Garcia “is not only about one man” but about Donald Trump's disregard of the American judicial system as well, Sen.
item.dc:creator: MARY CLARE JALONICK and ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: FSU shooting victims include a school employee whose dad was a Cuban exile turned CIA operative
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — Two people were killed and six others were injured when a gunman opened fire at Florida State University, sending students fleeing from the student union and putting the Tallahassee, Florida, campus under lockdown.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Appeals court halts Boasberg’s contempt proceedings against Trump administration
item.description: A divided federal appeals court panel on Friday temporarily halted U.S. District Judge James Boasberg’s contempt proceedings against the Trump administration over its deportation flights to El Salvador last month. The U.S. Court of Appeals for the D.C.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Fired probationary workers at Commerce say their insurance is being cut early
item.description: Probationary workers who were refired this month from the Commerce Department say their health insurance is being terminated earlier than they expected.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, NewsNation

item.title: Canadian drummer arrested on child sexual abuse material charges in California
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Joseph Seiders, drummer for Canadian indie rock band The New Pornographers, has been arrested and charged with possessing child sex abuse materials, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump taps Mark Levin, Henry McMaster to ‘revamped’ Homeland Security council
item.description: President Trump announced Thursday that he has picked Fox News contributor Mark Levin, South Carolina Gov. Henry McMaster (R) and other conservative allies to serve on a "revamped" Homeland Security Advisory Council (HSAC).
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Abrego Garcia told visiting US senator he was no longer being held at notorious Salvadoran prison
item.description: Maryland Sen. Chris Van Hollen was flying home from El Salvador on Friday after meeting with Kilmar Abrego Garcia, who was wrongly deported there by the Trump administration. It’s unclear what will happen next in the case.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Lawsuit challenges Trump administration crackdown on international students
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A class action lawsuit filed Friday asks a federal court to reinstate the legal status of international students caught up in a Trump administration crackdown that has left more than a thousand fearful of deportation.
item.dc:creator: ALIA WONG, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Slain Texas teen Austin Metcalf’s home swatted hours after tense press conference showdown
item.description: A swatting took place at Jeff Metcalf's home just hours after he was criticized by a spokesman for the family of his son's alleged murderer.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: For some, the Florida State University shooting is a grim repeat of the Parkland massacre
item.description: A few of the people who were at Florida State University for a deadly shooting were also traumatized by the Parkland massacre seven years ago.
item.dc:creator: Anthony Izaguirre And Stephany Matat, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Montana reports first measles cases since 1990
item.description: Two children have died amid the outbreak this year and a third death is currently under investigation.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra O'Connell-Domenech, KRON4

item.title: US has 800 measles cases and outbreaks in several states. Here's what you should know
item.description: The U.S. has 800 cases of measles nationwide as of Friday, and two more states identified outbreaks this week. Texas is driving the high numbers, with an outbreak centered in West Texas that started nearly three months ago and is up to 597 cases.
item.dc:creator: DEVI SHASTRI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Video shows doctor with measles treating kids. RFK Jr later praised him as an ‘extraordinary’ healer
item.description: PROVIDENCE, R.I. (AP) — A Texas doctor who has been treating children in a measles outbreak was shown on video with a measles rash on his face in a clinic a week before Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. met him and praised him as an “extraordinary” healer. Dr.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE R. SMITH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Supreme Court blocks, for now, new deportations under 18th century wartime law
item.description: The Supreme Court has blocked, for now, the deportations of any Venezuelans held in northern Texas under an 18th century wartime law.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Riccardi, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Join these viewing events to see bighorn sheep in their natural habitat
item.description: If you've ever wanted to catch a glimpse of bighorn sheep in the wild, you may want to check out these two viewing events hosted by the Utah Division of Wildlife Resources.
item.dc:creator: Kayla Baggerly, ABC 4

item.title: 2 food service employees killed in FSU shooting
item.description: A food service worker was identified as one of two people killed when a gunman opened fire on the Florida State University campus Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, ABC 4

item.title: Harvey Weinstein can stay in hospital during #MeToo retrial, judge rules
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Harvey Weinstein has been moved to a New York City hospital after a judge approved the ailing ex-studio boss’s request to stay there rather than in jail when he’s not in court for his #MeToo retrial.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trader Joe's plans to open 21 new locations across 13 states
item.description: Is your neighborhood lacking a grocery store that could easily double a well-lit tiki bar? That may change in the coming year.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, NewsNation

item.title: Military officials identify 2 Marines killed in crash
item.description: Two Camp Pendleton Marines killed in a crash during a convoy movement in New Mexico Tuesday have been identified.
item.dc:creator: Domenick Candelieri, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ryan Gosling to star in next 'Star Wars' film, Lucasfilm announces
item.description: The Force was with many Japanese, as well as visitors from abroad, at a “Star Wars” event on Friday where Lucasfilm announced the next installation in the film franchise.
item.dc:creator: Yuri Kageyama, KRON4

item.title: El Salvador president responds after Dem visits alleged MS-13 member and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump administration takes aim at Harvard's international students and tax-exempt status
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration has escalated its ongoing battle with Harvard, threatening to block the university from enrolling international students as the president called for withdrawing Harvard’s tax-exempt status.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA, FATIMA HUSSEIN and ALIA WONG, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Deer Valley Music Festival to feature Lindsey Stirling, Beach Boys, and Common
item.description: The 2025 Deer Valley Music Festival lineup has now been announced, featuring big names on the lineup — such as The Beach Boys, Lindsey Stirling, and Common.
item.dc:creator: Kayla Baggerly, ABC 4

item.title: Jury selection heads into next week at Weinstein rape retrial
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Dozens more people were screened Thursday as potential jurors for Harvey Weinstein's New York rape retrial, but the selection wrapped up for the week with several slots still to be filled.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Facebook sending out $40 payments to users over 'Like button' issue
item.description: Check your Venmo, email and mailbox: A payment from a years-old class action lawsuit against Facebook may be on its way this week.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, WFLA, NBC

item.title: San Francisco's new mayor is starting to unite the fractured city
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Democrats nationally may be in turmoil, but liberals in San Francisco are hopeful the new mayor's collaborative approach will help solve entrenched problems in a city recently known for its bitter infighting and chaotic streets.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR and OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Visa cancellations sow panic for international students, with more than 1,000 fearing deportation
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — At first, the bar association for immigration attorneys began receiving inquiries from a couple students a day. These were foreigners studying in the U.S., and they'd discovered in early April their legal status had been terminated with little notice.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA, MAKIYA SEMINERA and CHRISTOPHER L. KELLER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Menendez brothers hearing moves forward, albeit with confusion
item.description: The resentencing hearing for Eric and Lyle Menendez moved forward Thursday morning despite a motion for delay filed by Los Angeles County District Attorney Nathan Hochman.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, Fox 5

item.title: Kamala Harris holds lead in hypothetical California governor race, poll shows
item.description: Former Vice President Kamala Harris would enjoy a wide lead over the rest of the field if she were to enter the 2026 race for California governor, the results of a recent poll show.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KRON4

item.title: Officials in Puerto Rico demand answers after another blackout leaves 1.4 million without power
item.description: Crews worked early Thursday to restore power to Puerto Rico after a blackout across the entire island that affected the main international airport, several hospitals and hotels filled with Easter vacationers.
item.dc:creator: Dánica Coto, Fox 5

item.title: Republicans consider increasing taxes on the rich in break from party orthodoxy
item.description: Republicans in Congress are considering increasing taxes on the rich as a part of President Trump’s “big, beautiful bill” of ambitious legislative priorities, a striking development that breaks with decades of party orthodoxy and is spurring alarm bells from traditional conservatives.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, ABC 4

item.title: China-founded e-commerce sites Temu, Shein say they're raising prices due to tariffs
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — China-founded e-commerce sites Temu and Shein say they plan to raise prices for U.S.
item.dc:creator: MAE ANDERSON AP Business Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Handyman named person of interest after missing woman's body found buried in concrete
item.description: A missing woman was found buried and partially encased in concrete more than a week after she went missing. Now, deputies are questioning her handyman for his possible role in her disappearance and death.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, KTLA 5

item.title: DOGE targets a community service program in its latest cost-cutting effort
item.description: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — A 30-year-old community service program that sends young adults to work on projects across the U.S. was the latest target of the Trump administration 's campaign to slash government spending.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH FINGERHUT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Student accused in Dallas school shooting walked toward group in hall and opened fire, injuring 4
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — The student accused of injuring four in a shooting at a Dallas high school was let into the building through an unsecured door and then walked down a hallway toward a group of students, opening fire on them and then appearing to take a point-blank shot at one, according to an arrest
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, ABC 4
